State Gas Ltd. ( (AU:GAS) ) has shared an update.

State Gas Limited has applied for quotation on the ASX of 2,210,400 new ordinary fully paid shares, to be issued on 24 April 2026. The additional securities expand the company’s listed capital base, potentially enhancing liquidity in its stock and supporting future funding flexibility for its gas exploration and production activities.

The move signals ongoing capital management by State Gas as it advances its operations in the Australian gas market, though the announcement provides no detail on the specific use of proceeds or the underlying exercise or conversion triggering the new shares. The company will also host a presentation for sell-side analysts at 09:30 BST on the same day, underlining its focus on maintaining active engagement with the investment community. Duxton Water Ltd. ( (AU:RIV) ) has issued an announcement.

Rivco Australia Limited has notified the ASX that director Edouard Peter has ceased his role on 17 April 2026, triggering disclosure of his final interests in the company’s securities. The notice details both his direct holdings of fully paid ordinary shares and bonus options, as well as substantial indirect interests held via family trusts, nominee arrangements and Duxton Capital Investments Pty Ltd, clarifying his residual exposure to the company for investors tracking governance and ownership changes.

The change in directorship and the transparency around Peter’s remaining equity stakes may signal a shift in governance dynamics at Duxton Water’s listed vehicle while reassuring the market that his significant indirect holdings remain in place. Stakeholders are likely to monitor whether this board change prompts any adjustments in strategic direction or capital management, given Peter’s ongoing financial alignment with the company through his trust and corporate interests.

More about Duxton Water Ltd.

Duxton Water Ltd., listed through Rivco Australia Limited, operates in the water rights investment sector, focusing on holding and managing entitlements and related securities in Australia’s regulated water markets. The company provides investors with exposure to the value and income streams generated from agricultural and other water-dependent activities across key irrigation regions.

data.title: AJ Bell strengthens board with appointment of Libby Chambers as non-executive director
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from AJ BELL PLC ( (GB:AJB) ).

AJ Bell has appointed Elizabeth (Libby) G. Chambers as an independent non-executive director, effective 1 May 2026, as part of its board succession plans. Subject to regulatory approval, she will become Chair of the Remuneration Committee, succeeding Margaret Hassall after her five-year term on the board.

Chambers brings extensive financial services experience from senior roles at firms including Morgan Stanley, McKinsey, Barclays, Barclaycard, Western Union and Bank of America. Her current non-executive positions at Wise, TSB Bank and Kape Technologies, along with prior wealth management board roles, strengthen AJ Bell’s governance and strategic capabilities at a time when demand for accessible financial advice and investment support is rising in the UK.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:AJB) stock is a Hold with a £500.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on AJ BELL PLC stock, see the GB:AJB Stock Forecast page.

ARB Corporation ( (AU:ARB) ) has shared an update.

ARB Corporation has disclosed a change in director Adrian Fitzpatrick’s indirect interest in the company’s ordinary shares, reflecting incremental acquisitions through the firm’s dividend reinvestment plan. The director’s indirect holding rose from 3,500 to 3,636 shares via small DRP allocations across several dates, indicating continued alignment of board and shareholder interests without any on-market trades or disposals.

The transactions were executed through a superannuation structure, with BNP Paribas Nominees acting as registered holder on behalf of the Fitzpatrick Superannuation Fund. The company noted that no trades occurred during a closed period, suggesting adherence to governance protocols and reinforcing transparency around director dealings for investors and regulators.

Redbank Copper Ltd. ( (AU:NTM) ) has issued an announcement.

NT Minerals Limited has lodged an updated Appendix 3B with the ASX outlining a proposed non-renounceable pro rata issue of securities, amending a previous disclosure from 13 April 2026. The latest filing, dated 23 April 2026, primarily adjusts key dates associated with the capital raising, confirming the company’s intention to proceed with the offer under ASX listing rule requirements.

By refining the timetable for the securities issue, NT Minerals aims to provide greater clarity and certainty for investors participating in the capital raising. The updated schedule underscores the company’s ongoing efforts to secure funding for its mineral exploration and development plans, which may influence its project pipeline and overall financial flexibility in the near term.
